text_content: Varian utilizes a process based on the Rational Unified Process, and is defined in document reference DND01 Design and Development. This procedure defines the phases of a project, and more specifically, the various documentation requirements for each project phase to provide a high level of confidence in both the product, and the processes used to develop that product. The procedure further defines the level of management oversight necessary, in the form of Phase Gates at the end of each prescribed phase. The main purpose of the procedure is to comply with global regulatory requirements for design control, in particular US FDA 21 CFR 820, and ISO 13485. A second document, RSK01 Medical Device Risk Management is used to supplement DND01 and provides guidance on Risk Management consistent with EN ISO 14971:2019 Error! Reference source not found.. title: 3.0 Definitions > 4.2 Integrity > 4.2.2 Software Integrity:
text_content: During the design and development phases of the lifecycle for the application software within Varian medical devices, the integrity of the system build is ensured by controlling the editing process and requiring regular reviews of the code. This is done to ensure that the integrity of the application software is maintained through the construction phase. W hen the software package is ready for release to the user, Varian manufacturing procedures (DND1902) are used to prevent improper or incompatible versions of the software from being distributed or installed. These procedures include measures to ensure the consistency of data, the absence of viruses or malware, and the inclusion of needed user documentation and labeling. For Varian medical devices, installation procedures and updates are handled by trained Varian Service personnel or local Service representatives. title: 8.0 Production and Post-production Information
text_content: Production and post-production information will be continually monitored and analyzed to determine if new risks have been identified and to review the assessments of known risks to maintain an effective risk management process. This information includes, but is not limited to, design and process changes, revisions of standards, identifications of defects, and reports of complaints. In addition to Varian’s information about the device, the following shall be reviewed: • Information on similar devices on the market; • Publicly available information such as recalls, FDA Medical Device Reports, and vigilance reports; • Scientific literature. Analysis of relevant data is used to demonstrate that risk-based decisions and risk control measures for this device are appropriate and effective. text_content: Main control of content for accuracy, completeness, and quality is through reviews, internal and formal; UAD keystroking and tryout of new or changed features and procedures. Internal reviews are uncontrolled. They are intended to: Capture most changes before the formal review with longer review periods than the formal review. Ensure that all project team members have a chance to respond to the draft UA so that GPL can deliver the UA as planned. Resolve and reduce as many findings as possible before formal review. Formal reviews are controlled—findings are recorded—and require participation of the project team per RACI for Design and Development and GPL Authoring Process. Upgrades are performed per the information in ECs and DR and are documented in QNotes system. The DHR of this device is captured at the time of build, and before verification TRR. text_content: For the most rapid regulatory clearances internationally, the product name on the submission documents needs to match the name to be cleared and used in marketing the device. Upgrades and Conversions: Upgrades from a previous version to HAL 4.0 can be made in some countries where HAL 4.0 is cleared. The details of which countries allow this will be detailed later in the project. For China, the system updates cannot engage any re-labelling activities, the information on device label should not be changed, such as manufacturing date, model name, NMPA license number etc. . Conversions from Halcyon to Ethos radiotherapy sytem can be made in some countries. Conversions involve changes to the covers, installing and enabling the Ethos TPS (On-couch session manager or OSM) function and changing the nameplate to include “Ethos Radiotherapy System” while manufacturing date and serial number remain the same. The UDI will be updated to describe the changed model. Regulatory Plan Revision Doc Number

source: D40348 HAL 4.0 TSCL for SN 4 Release 4.00.0122 Rev14.pdf
title: 4.0 verification and validation testing.
text_content: Verification Testing Halcyon SN4 was built per Pre-production Phase I (Silver) DMR, and is maintained in the production equivalent configuration using Discrepancy Report (DR) Handling and Engineering Change (EC) and QNotes processes. Issues that are found during testing are logged as DRs, and the impact to verification and validation testing is assessed. This assessment defines whether the part needs to be replaced or reworked in the machines after implementation of the design change. This decision is documented in the DR. The hardware changes are implemented, and the final material dispositioning (per DR) is done via EC process. Upgrades are performed per the information in ECs and DR and are documented in QNotes system. The Device Parts List of the verification devices is captured at the time of build, and before verification TRR. Validation Testing Halcyon SN4 was upgraded to the final production equivalent configuration (defined by Gold DMR).

text_content: Production and post-production information will be continually monitored and analyzed to determine if new risks have been identified and to review the assessments of known risks to maintain an effective risk management process. Analysis of relevant data is used to demonstrate that risk-based decisions and risk control measures for this device are appropriate and effective. text_content: Varian utilizes a process based on the Rational Unified Process, and is defined in document reference DND01 Design and Development. This procedure defines the phases of a project, and more specifically, the various documentation requirements for each project phase to provide a high level of confidence in both the product, and the processes used to develop that product. The procedure further defines the level of management oversight necessary, in the form of Phase Gates at the end of each prescribed phase. The main purpose of the procedure is to comply with global regulatory requirements for design control, in particular US FDA 21 CFR 820, and ISO 13485. A second document, RSK01 Medical Device Risk Management is used to supplement DND01 and provides guidance on Risk Management consistent with EN ISO 14971:2019 Error! Reference source not found..
